webui_ask_user_tool.signature: @tool('webui_ask_user', 'Ask the user a structured multi-choice question', schema) async def webui_ask_user(args) -> ToolResult
webui_ask_user_tool.description: In-process SDK MCP tool handling structured questions (free-form text OR N-choice OR N>4 questions that built-in AskUserQuestion can't handle). Mints a server-side prompt_id, persists to agent_prompts, emits SSE ask_user event, awaits POST /api/agents/runs/{id}/answer with timeout. Called by skill prose when RAPID_RUN_MODE=sdk.

ask_free_text_tool.signature: @tool('ask_free_text', 'Prompt the user for free-form text input', schema) async def ask_free_text(args) -> ToolResult
ask_free_text_tool.description: Specialization of webui_ask_user for free-form text where the 4-question multi-choice limit of built-in AskUserQuestion doesn't fit.

render_diff_tool.signature: @tool('render_diff', 'Render a unified diff in the browser for user approval', schema) async def render_diff(args) -> ToolResult
render_diff_tool.description: Optional tool for surfacing unified diffs before destructive edits; reuses approval modal infrastructure.

ask_user_built_in_interception.signature: in can_use_tool: if tool_name == 'AskUserQuestion': if len(questions) > 4: split into multiple prompt_ids; emit ask_user event per batch; await all answers
ask_user_built_in_interception.description: Interception logic in can_use_tool that catches built-in AskUserQuestion tool uses, splits >4 questions into multiple batches (SDK limit), routes each batch through EventBus. Zero skill changes required for CLI compatibility.

agent_prompts_model.signature: class AgentPrompt(SQLModel, table=True): id: str (prompt_id); run_id: str (FK); kind: Literal['ask_user','permission_req','approve_tool']; payload: str (json); status: Literal['pending','answered','expired','stale']; answer: str | None; created_at: datetime; answered_at: datetime | None
agent_prompts_model.description: Server-minted prompt per round-trip. Prevents race (wrong answer applied to wrong question) via id-keyed acknowledgement. 409 returned on stale prompt_id; timeout auto-expires pending prompts.

answer_endpoint.description: Frontend submits the answer for a pending prompt. Backend validates prompt_id matches current pending prompt for run; rejects stale answers.

approval_modal_component.description: React component with Approve / Reject / Edit triad. Focus-trapped, ESC=reject, default=reject for destructive patterns. Edit mode allows modifying tool args before approving (e.g. change a file path).

ask_user_modal_component.description: React component rendering structured questions (multi-choice, free-form, N-questions) with sessionStorage-backed draft persistence across tab close.

permission_prompt_component.description: Specialization of ApprovalModal for permission_req events (when can_use_tool routes unknowns to user).

skill_prose_patches.signature: Conditional skill-prose branches in 9 SKILL.md files based on $RAPID_RUN_MODE env var
skill_prose_patches.description: Minimal edits to skill files (discuss-set, init, new-version discovery half, bug-fix, scaffold, branding, quick scoping, assumptions, add-set mid-milestone). In sdk mode, call mcp__rapid__webui_ask_user; otherwise retain built-in AskUserQuestion. No file forking.

build_sdk_options.description: Needed to register our MCP tools via create_sdk_mcp_server() on ClaudeAgentOptions.mcp_servers

can_use_tool_hook.signature: async def can_use_tool(tool_name, tool_args, context) -> ToolPermissionDecision
can_use_tool_hook.description: We extend can_use_tool to intercept built-in AskUserQuestion. Hook point is owned by foundation; interception logic lives here.

EventBus.description: We publish ask_user and permission_req events via EventBus

sse_event_schema.signature: 'ask_user' and 'permission_req' event kinds with stable payload shapes
sse_event_schema.description: Our events ride on the typed SSE event schema

agent_run_model.description: agent_prompts.run_id is an FK to agent_runs.id

rapid_run_mode_env.description: Skill-prose patches branch on $RAPID_RUN_MODE; the foundation must set this for SDK runs

create_sdk_mcp_server_integration.description: We register our @tool functions through this integration point

prompt_id_is_server_minted.description: Every ask_user or permission_req emits a server-side UUID prompt_id. Frontend cannot invent one. Stale prompt_id from old tab yields 409.

four_question_split_enforced.description: Built-in AskUserQuestion with >4 questions is split server-side into multiple prompts before being emitted. Skills that batch 12 gray areas (e.g. discuss-set) work transparently.

timeout_on_pending_prompt.description: A pending agent_prompts row older than per-skill answer_timeout (default 30 minutes) is marked 'expired' and the agent run enters 'waiting' -> 'interrupted' state. UI surfaces 'this run timed out'.

cli_parity_additive_only.description: Skill-prose patches use if/else branches on $RAPID_RUN_MODE, never forking the skill file. CLI run (mode != 'sdk') uses built-in AskUserQuestion exactly as before. Regression CI test runs /rapid:execute-set via CLI against fixture every build.

approval_modal_destructive_default_rejec.description: When permission_req includes a destructive tool (Bash with rm/git push --force/branch -D regex match), the approval modal defaults to reject; user must explicitly click Approve. ESC still equals reject.

draft_persists_across_tab_close.description: AskUserModal's in-progress answer is saved to sessionStorage keyed by prompt_id. Tab close + reopen restores draft. Server status is truth if the prompt was answered elsewhere.
